description: A dual-axis analysis framework for systematically researching products, companies, concepts, or people. The vertical axis traces the full lifecycle; the horizontal axis maps the current competitive landscape.

subject.name: string — Name of the research subject
subject.type: enum: product | company | concept | person | other
subject.one_line_definition: string — One sentence that says exactly what this thing is
subject.researcher: string — Who is conducting this research
subject.research_motivation: string — Why research this? What was the trigger?

vertical_axis_lifecycle.description: The full timeline from origin to present, told as narrative — the more detailed and multifaceted, the better

origin.birth_context: string — The era, industry state, and technological environment at the time of birth
origin.birth_motivation: string — Why did it appear? What problem did it solve? What gap did it fill?

founder_or_initiator.background: string — What had these people done before, and why were they the ones to do this
founder_or_initiator.initial_vision: string — What they originally set out to build

origin.early_form: string — What it looked like initially and how it differs from today
origin.seed_event: string — Was there a key event or inspiration that directly sparked its creation

key_milestones_timeline.why_it_matters: string — How this event altered the subsequent trajectory
key_milestones_timeline.decisions_and_tradeoffs: string — What choices were made at this node, and what was given up
key_milestones_timeline.external_trigger: string | null — Whether triggered by external events (policy, competitor moves, market shifts)
key_milestones_timeline.outcome_and_impact: string — Direct and indirect consequences of this event

phase_breakdown.phase_name: string — e.g.: nascent, rapid growth, transformation, maturity
phase_breakdown.defining_characteristic: string — The most prominent feature of this phase
phase_breakdown.central_tension: string — The biggest challenge or internal tension during this phase
key_figures: string — People who played a critical role in this phase
key_decisions: string — Major decisions made during this phase
phase_breakdown.phase_outcome: string — What scorecard this phase delivered by its end

lock_in_decisions: string — Which early decisions locked in later directions and became hard to reverse
missed_forks: string — Which critical moments could have gone a different way
path_dependency_analysis.self_reinforcing_mechanisms: string — What mechanisms drove ever-deeper commitment (network effects, ecosystem lock-in, user habits, etc.)

narrative_threads.main_storyline: string — If the entire arc had to be captured in one story, what would it be
recurring_themes: string — Patterns, tensions, or themes that recur across phases
turning_points: string — The most dramatic turning points
open_questions: string — What information is still missing and needs further investigation

horizontal_axis_competitive_comparison.description: A systematic horizontal comparison with competitors/peers on the current time-slice
horizontal_axis_competitive_comparison.analysis_time_slice: date — The reference point in time for horizontal comparison

subject_profile.current_positioning: string — How it defines itself today
subject_profile.business_model: string — How it makes money / sustains itself
subject_profile.user_scale_and_growth: string — Current scale and growth trend

typical_use_cases: string — Primary scenarios where users deploy it

competitor_list.competitor_type: enum: direct competitor | indirect competitor | potential competitor | alternative solution
competitor_list.key_difference_from_subject: string — The most fundamental distinction compared to the research subject
competitor_list.threat_analysis: string — Why it poses this level of threat

dimension_comparison_matrix.description: Pick the most critical dimensions and line up the subject and all competitors for comparison

comparison_dimensions.dimension_name: string — e.g.: performance, pricing, usability, ecosystem, community activity

competitive_landscape_assessment.current_landscape: string — Is it a free-for-all, duopoly, monopoly, or something else
competitive_landscape_assessment.why_this_landscape: string — Why the landscape looks this way
competitive_landscape_assessment.landscape_trajectory: string — Where the landscape is heading next
competitive_landscape_assessment.subject_position: string — Where the subject sits in this landscape

cross_axis_insights.description: The intersection of vertical and horizontal axes — the most valuable part of HV Analysis
cross_axis_insights.how_history_shaped_current_position: string — Which decisions and events in the vertical arc determined today's horizontal position
cross_axis_insights.competitor_vertical_comparison: string — If competitors are placed on a timeline too, how their origins and evolutions differ
historical_roots_of_current_strengths: string — Each current strength traced back to a historical node
historical_roots_of_current_weaknesses: string — Each current weakness traced back to a historical decision

future_projection.projection_from_vertical_trends: string — If current trajectory continues, what happens next
future_projection.projection_from_horizontal_competition: string — How the evolving competitive landscape affects the subject

output_requirements.vertical_output: Present as narrative/story — not a chronological bullet list. Must have causal logic, detail, characters, so readers feel 'how it got here step by step'
output_requirements.horizontal_output: Present as comparative analysis — structured dimensional comparison plus qualitative judgments and insights
output_requirements.cross_axis_output: Present as analytical commentary — weave information from both axes together to produce original judgments
output_requirements.suggested_word_count: Vertical 3,000–8,000 words, Horizontal 2,000–5,000 words, Cross-Axis Insights 1,000–2,000 words
output_requirements.source_attribution: All key facts must cite information sources (links, documents, interviews, etc.)
